item.text: I found the drop point: `story-verifier.ts` currently returns early on any batch error and never includes the successful sibling result. I’m reading the surrounding types and the CLI test patterns now so the fix lines up with the existing result envelope instead of inventing a new shape.

I’ve confirmed the contract already supports what we need: the batch `result` can carry `verifierResults` and `errors` at the same time, and CLI status derives separately. I’m patching the aggregator so mixed-success batches return a blocked envelope with the surviving structured verifier result preserved, then I’ll lock it in with the regression test.

item.text: There’s one subtle contract wrinkle here: the current batch-result schema insists `result.outcome` must match only the surviving verifier recommendations, which would reject a mixed-failure blocked batch. I’m checking that path now and I’ll adjust the implementation and test together so the public JSON stays internally consistent.

I’ve got the shape pinned down now. The fix is going in as a small pair: preserve successful `verifierResults` during mixed failures in `story-verifier.ts`, and relax the batch-result contract just enough to allow a blocked batch outcome when partial verifier evidence is all we have.

The code path is patched; I hit a small context mismatch while inserting the regression test, so I’m reopening the exact nearby section and dropping the new test into the right slot rather than forcing it.
The regression test is in place now. I’m running the focused verifier tests first to catch any contract mismatch quickly before I kick off the repo-level baseline and full green gate you asked for.

item.text:
Files changed: `processes/impl-cli/core/story-verifier.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/core/result-contracts.ts`, and `processes/impl-cli/tests/story-verify-command.test.ts`.

I updated story verification so mixed-success/mixed-failure batches still return `outcome: "block"` and `status: "blocked"`, but now preserve any successful sibling verifier payload in `result.verifierResults` while keeping failed-verifier details in `errors`. I also added a CLI regression test covering the case where one verifier subprocess crashes and the other returns a valid structured `revise` result with findings and requirement coverage.

Tests added: 1 regression test in `processes/impl-cli/tests/story-verify-command.test.ts` for partial verifier failure with preserved surviving evidence.

Gate result: `bun run capture:test-baseline --force` passed, and `bun run green-verify` passed (`171 pass, 0 fail`).
